title: Trachypogon stenophyllus
taxonomy: Trachypogon stenophyllus belongs to the family Poaceae. This genus is a type of grass and is commonly known as the "thatch grasses." Trachypogon stenophyllus is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a perennial grass with narrow leaves, typically found in wetland areas. The classification of Trachypogon stenophyllus is further divided into the subfamily Panicoideae.

intro: Trachypogon stenophyllus, also known as the narrow-leaved vetiver grass, is a type of grass native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, due to its calming and soothing effects on the nervous system. The bioactive constituents of Trachypogon stenophyllus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trachypogon stenophyllus are teas and infusions, which can be consumed to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
